text: I have been with T-Mobile since 2002, the service used to be cheap with a bad/mediocre network that went down often. When John Legere took over as CEO, the network improved and T-Mobile offered great prices, the two lines for $100 plan that I had was great. One year ago, someone in my household called the Manilla call center and ordered a new iPhone on my account, the foreign call center never questioned the person if they were me, all they focused on was the sale. I did get the household member to pay off the iPhone and I cancelled service with T-Mobile because I believed they did not care about their customers anymore after John Legere left in 2020. I went back to T-Mobile in June of 2021 because Verizon was having problems in the Los Angeles area. The service doesn't work in some popular areas in California, and I feel like T-Mobile has gone back in time to 2009/2010 when the service was terrible, with 10 MPS speeds. The call center in Manilla has little power to do anything and generally don't do much to resolve any problems. My team of experts used to be in Oregon and they were easy to get ahold of before the call center staff was outsourced to Manilla. T-Mobile promised that they would hire thousands of people in the USA, instead they are hiring more outsourced people in Manilla. Now when I call Manilla, the staff there are clueless as to what is happening on the T-Mobile network. My hope is that T-Mobile will improve the customer experience, it is terrible right now.

Any stars are too many.  Signed up for home internet gateway after being told that there was great service at my address.  In reality there was intermittent and poor service.  After many hours with tech support it was determined that there really wasn’t any reliable service.  I canceled the service and was told that the charges for this account would be refunded.  Although many calls to customer service confirmed that I would be refunded, T-Mobile never followed through with the refund.  In fact they threatened me after I contested the credit card charge.  I filed a complaint with the BBB, but like all scum bag companies,  T-Mobile isn’t a member.  Customer Service agents want and try to help,  but corporate flunkies seem to override them.
T-Mobile network is so terrible now. Can no longer get service anyway now,  not even in places I used to get service.
Absolutely the worst customer service. I’ve wasted countless hours. Don’t use them if you have any other option.

T-mobile's customer service sucks.
I was very unsatisfied with the service I received at a t-mobile store
 on 12th Market streets in Philadelphia. I had an appointment  to get my Galaxy watch fixed. The lady employee that waited on me had no knowledge of how to fix my watch nor did she call anyone to assist. She kinda rushed me out the store and told me to call Sprint. I was happy when I went into the store today and very sad when I left.

T-Mobile took advantage of a old woman with dementia. They continue to take her money and have never once provided her a service. T-Mobile won’t talk to me(her son) to get reimbursement for the many months the steal money from her account. And since she has dementia she can’t help herself. 
SHAME ON YOU T-Mobile

The ease of doing business or even to cancel your account with T-Mobile is horrible. Not worth the savings! Stay with your current provider!! I had no service after switching, could not make any calls, and when I went to cancel, I requested cancellation and even after 19 days, still they couldn't cancel my account!!!! I requested a cancellation verbally and in writing daily for 19 days and still took money out of my bank account! Just not a pleasant experience at all.

Couldn't be more pleased with the sales representatives.  Both Henley & Ruth couldn't have done more as they both were well informed; giving us the information we needed & answering our questions. Thanks to both!!!
T-Mobile is trash..don’t waste your money. Coverage suck, calls drop and the only time you get 4 to 5G is when you in city limits. Phone doesn’t work too good closed in you be lucky to get 2bars 😞

I have had T Mobile for a few years and recently experienced problems with reception at my home. I have replaced SIM cards, etc. and according to the store the only way to contact them is with the "611" number. Bottom line I have called 6+ times, contacted them via web site and their customer service is horrible, period!!

T-Mobile just ripped of my mother… elderly, disabled and low income. All she wanted was a robo call blocker for her land line!! I’ve been on the phone for hours with T-Mobile trying to cancel a new account… they’re located “out of the country”,  got hung up on 3x, NO ONE WILL HELP ME!! Then I called the bank, cancelled the card! But the bank is still allowing the $15 charge for the “free” land line they sold to my mother —they don’t have land lines!!! for $40/month.., oh and a bunch of equipment and a new phone to go through! How did they get my mother who doesn’t know how to send an email to sign an electronic contract ???!!  For the love of all mothers!!! Do not use T-Mobile !! Cancel now! Run for your life!!
This have been the worst experience I hade ever hade with a phone company I will not ever use T-Mobile after this

This is one of the worst companies I have ever dealt with in my lifetime. They are overcharging me and I have spent countless hours trying to "prove" they need to honor their promotion and ever time I speak with a representative they have some new item the "need" that they didn't last time. This company is a bunch of thieves. Warning; DO NOT USE T MOBILE or expect them to honor any of their promotions. You will most certainly regret it.

I have to make a review about this T-Mobile company.  If I only knew about how excellent their customer service are, I would have switched to them a long time ago.  I only been with this company this year and so far, they have given me a lot of wonderful perks considering I only a prepaid customer.  I wish I can remember all of the customer service staff I have spoken to re: my account, the latest one was Jorge (1878975), he was so helpful to me.  He was very patient and took care of my concerns re: travel abroad and connections.  What a wonderful customer service staff.  I am giving him 5 stars for taking care of me and my t-mobile needs.  You guys deserve recognition for your excellent service.

Absolutely splendid people at TMobile on Erie Blvd in Syracuse, NY. Stephanie transformed my service package, cutting my bill in half. Set us up with new iPhones, all at reduced $$$. Great positive people who know their stuff.

Worst customer service! 1st I was never happy with their coverage. I live in Southwest Florida, poor service had to use WiFi calling using their signal booster. After transferring my service to another provider. There was a negative balance of $149.33 due to me. Have been trying to get it refunded for past 6 months. Every time I call them, get different explanation.
After waiting for almost an hour,I got a call back, still same BS answer. So I asked the agent to transfer me to a supervisor, he asked why, I said because I am not getting satisfactory answers, He hung up on me

Not impressed. Pathetic service in the Kingsport, TN store. Even worse on the phone. Wife needed a new phone. We were moved from Sprint to tmobile when they merged. Went to our local store to get her a new phone. The children working there would not show or advise of phone features or show her a phone without a struggle. Ended TMobile after a discussion with their customer service. Even worse there. Couldn't speak or understand English. Couldn't even escalate to a supervisor. Ended that uselessness and right back to Verizon immediately.

I used to be with sprint…decent…then comes T-Mobile.  SOOO many problems, mostly with coverage.  But the customer service is useless and sometimes rude and now it turns out i have to pay to leave!!!     T-Mobile sucks ass.

Simply wanted to do a 55+ 2line offer. It took many hours over several days of confusion. The store I went to was run with deception , if not lies. Beware the offer of netflix on us. I could not use that since I have netflix streaming and DVD already. I would have to cancel my DVD service to get the credit. Customer service means nothing to them. One of the worst companies i have dealt with. DO NOT USE!!!!!

Currently on an hour-long wait with T-Mobile to transfer my phone number from Verizon. They gave no instruction on how to transfer a line or really on anything else. As I'm discovering how awful their customer service, I go on the Verizon site and talk with a live agent within a minute. In 5 minutes she helped me with staying with Verizon. She said I just have to cancel when the T-Mobile agent picks up.

So. TERIBBLE customer service and terrible guidance.

Falsely created an account under my wife’s name In store after we decided to go elsewhere. To which we never received a phone, contract, emails or any paperwork. Checking the credit card statement at the end of the month and there’s an account from t-mobile deducting $70. Called and called spent hours on the phone, they kept saying we needed to return to store, the store said Its not our problem you need to ring customer service again. This has been going on for 3 months now. They have taken a further $70 but assured me they closed the account and would refund the $140… still waiting after another phone call today with a 3rd “manager” about refunds but not holding my breath. Credit card company has been informed of fraud against my account! 
T-mobile are a waste of space!

On going problem! No one knows how to fix anything. The store (in casa grande az) sold me a watch with its own line and it never worked. I paid for this for 6 months before I discovered it only worked by BT. When I brought it to their attention they replied "you're 1/2 sprint and 1/2 t-mobile!" Nothing we can do! I had to fight to get reimbursed and never was fully reimbursed. I've spent over 50 hrs in 6 months on the phone with support. Who trains these people? I've been a sprint customer since 2009. Now I'll be looking to switch and contacting the news for help.

So for years I used Lyca mobile a discount provider. Lyca was 40/ mo for unlimited talk/text/data. The claimed to throttle speeds after 25Gb. However I regularly use 40-50Gb and never noticed any slowdown. And in 4 yrs of service I had Zero problems. Everything worked all of the time. Even my billing was always accurate and predictable!  I had no idea how wonderful that was until moved.
When Ioved to edge of town my Lyca service had no tower close enough and the phone fiction quit working. So I switched to Verizon thinking they had tower close by. I was mistaken, the Verizon was just as bad as Lyca except they could not even get bills correct and staff were difficult.
After talking neighbors found out the tower is a T mobile tower. So I switched to T-Mobile. While the actual service is great, meaning the phone works and data is great!
However, I have memory issues, thought the Iphone would help. So I bought one. It was great for 3 days then it connected to apple update and started asking for passwords I did not remember. I tried to get help from T -mobile and apple with poor results. They managed to get phone locked up. In frustration I returned the phone, clearly I  mo longer smart enough for Apple products. Disappointed. The packaging said I must return phone through a store since it had to inspected. Fine. I take it to a T-Mobile store, manager days it must be mailed in? HE PREPARES A RETURN LABEL AFFIXES IT TO PACKAGE!
I take package and mail it off via UPS dropbox in parking lot. I filmed my inserting package in Ups box!.
3 days later I receive text saying they received phone,!  Now it is 3 months later, they claim they can't find phone,
I have then tracking number, video of me putting it in box, etc. Still being billed for item I don't have! Have not received back my down payment. I am s poor man. I am now hungry and need money back for food! So I guess T- Mobile is forcing me to go on Hunger Strike against company. 
The worst part,? I am a peaceful loving compassionate person. So 
yesterday, I found myself literally screaming at the T-Mobile"expert"and almost threatening them! That sucks!
Especially since I truly believe the workers want to be helpful "experts" but the company obviously does not empower them to actually be experts.
They are, friendly, polite, cheerful, but can do nothing to actually help you aside from being comforting and saying no worries, we got your back, the problem will be corrected. When in fact nothing happens.  I feel trapped as this is only tower close enough to provide good signal. I guess I have to hire a lawyer to get my money back! I would strongly suggest avoiding T-Mobile. While friendly, the customer service sucks, useless, all they care about is polling, and getting a good review. Well if you want a good review you have to provide good service. You have great people, let them be great, empower them to help. And give me back my money. I consider this theft. You are thieves. I am hungry and need my money to eat! Give it back now.!
TMOBILE IS TERRIBLE! Calls drop, no service, they take money out of your account when you don't owe and they NEVER DO WHAT THEY SAY THEY WILL!! DON'T WASTE YOUR TIME!!

I've been with T-Mobile for over 20 years. The company has service perks like free international roaming that is great for someone who travels often. But the past two times I have called customer service it literally feels like I'm talking to someone they just hired off the street with zero training - horrible English, slow, no idea what is going on or what I'm saying, unable to resolve the issue. The first call was actually about a dangerous situation where I was in a complete blackout situation, lost service, electricity out in the whole area, no GPS, no idea how to get home. I was able to find wifi through someone's hotspot to call customer service but hung up after 30 mind of what felt like talking to someone slow in the head (no offense but it was frustrating and scary). Now today - similar experience after being on a wait line for 2(!) hours! What happened to you T-Mobile? So disappointed!

I'm very disappointed with T-Mobil and Sprint. I decide to buy my phone at the Sprint store because the manager told me I can upgrade my phone as soon they have a new model for free and my lease was 12 months when T-Mobil bought a Sprint, they change everything the lease right now is 18 months and if I wanna buy the new model I have to make a down-payment, this is the first think, I don't know how many times I called them to fix my monthly bill every month is so stressful for me. I'll never referral anyone to use those services, they don't care about customer just about money they don't even ask me if I'm satisfied with my service. You guys have to review your quality process to make a better business.

The T-mobile "exports" could not solve my billing issues for the past three months. Spend more than 30 hours called and chatted with them. They all said they will "personally follow up and track the issues" to make sure the issue are resolved. But, they all failed.

I have lived in 3 different countries and I have never seen a worst phone company. Expensive, lack of reception everywhere, website is slow, not easy...

I cannot say nothing good about this company

starting at $60 per month. I live in Rhode island pretty small State switched to t- mobile a few months ago .I am so disappointed in the 5g lead on catch 22 no 5g coverage in the state at all this company and all it's merger partners sprint , Metro pcs, all scamming people at there finger tips I had to Google it to see that there is absolutely no Towers with cells of 5g just the 5 or 6 major states LA, Chicago, New York lte 4g only sad sad @(#-#)@ 0 stars had to ad 1

Upgrade to the iPhone 13 Pro ON US up to $1000 on eligible trade in. 
ON US. In the United States, ON US means free. Drinks are ON US means we pay. The ad implies that current customers, such as myself, can upgrade to the iPhone 13 Pro FOR FREE, because it is on T-mobile. (FREE to me) This is deceptive, in other words, FALSE ADVERTISING. Having an iPhone 8 to trade in gets me $85 (in monthly bill credits over 30 months). That is no where near $1000 and in no way is this upgrade going to be FREE to me. I currently have 4 phones, 2 Smart watches and an iPad on the account and have been with T-mobile for many years. Imagine my disappointment. :(
Now, this evening, I see a tv ad for NEW customers, no trade-in required, simply port your number from your current carrier over to T-Mobile and get the iPhone 12 ON US. This tells me that NEW customers can get the iPhone 12 for FREE. What can I do to upgrade to the iPhone 12 as a current customer???  I would have to pay for the phone. Even at half price, which the representative on the phone implied was a legit offer, this is not FREE and not fair to long-term T-Mobile customers.
I have canceled the pre-order for the iPhone 13 Pro.
I am more than dissatisfied with T-mobile. And THAT is ON THEM!

there is something really wrong with this company. so unorganized hiring the wrong people rude inept incompetent. disconnected from the customers. charging me for an account that was closed and never used??? still months later adding fees and late charges now. was told it was closed and no credit card charges would be done to me. right. they lied apparently and did not do their jobs. seems so easy but not for this company. no they cant see i have no sim card or no usage but still charging me anyway??? infuriating. another poorly run company. try to  get thru to them no way and they hang up on you repeatedly. this world is so messed up now. this is a perfect example of  the crap we have to deal with. this company needs to be shut down. i use at and t.

*WARNING*- Cancel autopay when deciding to leave T- Mobile and going with another provider. They will standby with auto pay and make sure to deduct the full amount left on your bill after canceling. Meaning cost of phones and etc. Alot of people leave this company due to infractions of network coverage, Drop calls in locations they calm is supported by low or high band network. To hold cell phone companys more accountable for there terrible and flase advertisement service, Pay them back nice and slow. And keep them on the radar to get publicity. !!

To start with they were fine and gave me a free 15 days or some such number to try it out in my area. They sent me a couple of cards to put in my phone for the trial period. All good so far. I installed one card in my phone and during that day experienced 3 dropped calls, several poor signal situations, Decided to not try switching because I had not experienced these issues before with Verizon.  I called them to cancel, they couldn't do that over the phone, so had to go into a store a few miles away. They were nice enough and promised to cancel it for me, but then I began to get bills in the mail! I went in again and they again promised to take care of it, but I still get bills every month. I'm afraid they will ding my credit score so am very unhappy with the lack of carry through and will continue to put reviews wherever I can to keep others from the same abuse. I warned them that I would do this... but apparently they don't care. I do feel sorry for the sales people that are stuck in such a company!

While the service is good for how we use it, there are arbitration lawsuits against it. 
In my personal experience the sales people either online or at the store do not disclose the full details of the FREE phones or BUY ONE GET ONE FREE.
They are big on saying they don't have contracts but unless you pay up front they make you tied to them for up to three years. 
The best thing to do would be to go over line per line the contract your signing with the salesperson.

So TMobile is offering free iPhone 12 to new customers. I understand they want more and more business. Yet their loyal existing customers get nothing but a monthly payment if they want a new phone. It is like joining a club and paying full dues and the the club offers zero dues memberships to non members. You are bound to upset a lot of people. I will soon be leaving TMobile!

I recently heard about personal info being breached at T mobile on many T mobile customers. Let's talk about T mobiles association with it disastrous phone insurance company. I've posted this all over Facebook and intend to do it weekly as well as other media platforms. T mobile sells you insurance with Assurant to protect damaged ,lost or stolen phones. I have spent 6 days trying to get a phone replaced with Assurant and 3 days with a T mobile rep on line while doing it. Assurant gets your password you set up with T mobile so you've been verified. But then they want a picture ID ,passport,or driver's license sent to them. Remember folks these documents now fill it the blanks for hackers ,because like most people you have your banking and credit card  information on your phone or home computer.  Here's your back door leak. I asked Assurant representatives if they would accept liability if my info was compromised and caused me any reprocusions financially or professionally.  They would not. T mobile stop getting customer insurance with Assurant.  I'm posting this on every media site I can weekly forever. David klein 
ending in 6275

Tmobile lied to me about getting a refund 3 times. I called them because I accidentally paid a bill that is not due yet. Customer represantives promise me that I will get the refund in 1- 3 business days, she even asked for my checking account number and routing so she can expidite my refund. In 3 days I got an email that  my account is not qualified refund. They did this twice!!! This company is full of crap! Be careful when using their payment online! They always mislead customers in paying more. Very tricky. I am moving to another company very soon.

Buggy software they keep pushing out to my phone.  Can't remove it.  Anything TMobile makes is crap.  It's not surprising that people keep breaking into T-Mobiles servers and stealing customer data.  Their apps are junk do their security is the same.  Finally left this two bit company

edits.text: I came across these reviews and had to leave one for myself.  I've been a TMobile customer since 2013.  I have nothing but good things to say about them.  Customer service is the best I've ever delt with.  They know what they're talking about and have resolved the few minor issues that I've encountered over the past eight years.  My bills are always correct.  If I could rate them 100 stars I would

text: Worthless service it's been down for over a month can't even check the weather paying up the ying yang and we get no service and then it finally barely works and they tell me all my dada  is gone. I'm going to get home internet and I'm canceling these pieces of s***phone plan T-Mobile you suck you're way worse than Verizon and I'm disgusted with this worthless service I can't even get a phone call a mile out of town horrible I will be getting hold of you to get this dealt with which is almost impossible cuz we get no help from you people.

T-Mobile used to have the best service before they merged with Sprint. Maybe they want to only work with brand new 5G phones. Phones that only do H+ ( HSPA ) and 4G LTE no longer get signal in many parts of the country. I will probably switch carriers so I have a usable phone. I plan to upgrade my 2-year-old phone to the Pixel 6 when it comes out later this year but I am not confident that it will work with T-Mobile either. I've always used Google phones because they have the least extra added software.

There area where I have problems is in St Augustine Florida, Atlanta Georgia and l-75 going from St Augustine to Atlanta. Most of my issues are data issues. My phone is supposed to provide data for Android Auto and I also use Android Auto for my in car music. My phone issues are mostly in St Augustine where I would not be able to make a phone call from my home if my phone did not use my home internet service to make the phone call. They said there was supposed to be a new tower for southern St Augustine on July 4th but I saw no improvement.
T-Mobile's new website really sucks big time. Need to change to another provider. Also, has very poor coverage. I heard Verizon is better.

DO NOT SWITCH TO T-MOBILE. They are an extremely deceptive company. They convinced me to switch to Verizon (after 20 years of being a Verizon customer) by offering a promotion where I got an ~$800 credit for my old phone applied to a new phone, essentially getting the new phone for free. The credit would be applied monthly for the next 24 months. I then saw my bill and the credit was NOT applied. I called and they said they only gave me $75 credit, not $800, because my phone had a small crack on the screen. The salesperson NEVER told me the screen had to be flawless. I don't even remember there being a crack either. T Mobile never called me, emailed me, or mailed me about this. I found out about it from noticing my bill was too high. I contacted customer service and they claimed there was nothing they could do about it. I was lied to and cheated out of over $700. Has this happened to anyone else? wondering if there can be a class action lawsuit.

Worst experience ever. Everyday I receive a threatening email about excess late charges, etc etc. on a device for which all charges and fees have been waived. Apparently. It takes 2 months for their system to acknowledge they screwed up but they have already turned my service off once ..a phone call and a 2 hour wait restored it..but my son is military and needs his communication device.

My first cell phone service was through T-mobile a LOOOONG time ago. I was with them for 10 years. Loved their service and how they treated us. I had to leave T-Mobile because I moved to an area that wasn't well supported with towers and cell service was very bad. I was actually sad. I vowed to go back once they built up their towers in my city. That time was now. 
End of July, I signed up online. I was going to try out their Internet service, along with a cell plan. The Tmobile associate lied to get the sale. She said my router would be shipped the next day. She "hooked me up" with free accessories. But, she set me up for apple accessories. I repeated that I had a Galaxy and she said "That's ok. Just take the stuff to your local store to exchange them". I told her to just forget about it. But, she had them sent anyway. 
The next day, I got a notice that the router was on back order with no expected date. I called in to customer service and got passed around to 5 different people before they hung up on me. I called back, this person was CLUELESS. Got hung up on again. Called a third time and after 3 different people I was told the router wouldn't be available until at least October. Then, I found out the accessories sent WEREN'T free. So, I told them I wanted to cancel. Got passed off again and hung up on. 
I went to the local store to cancel and the local associate got hung up on twice and the 3rd person we got was so dumb, she locked herself out of the system and we had to end the call. Ridiculous!!
I finally went to a different local store across town and HE GOT HUNG UP ON twice before we finally got the service canceled. 
Fast forward 3 weeks and T-Mobile has sent me a bill for equipment I never got and the accessories that were sent back. I was on hold for 45 minutes to straighten this BS out. Once again, they make me go to the local store to do this. Guy at the store is on hold for 30 minutes, but T-Mobile finally confirms my stuff is sent back and my acct is at ZERO. 2 weeks later, I get a collections bill. ffs. Go back to the main TM store and the manager calls in and the person he gets argues with him about the entire situation despite the fact that there are notes in my *now canceled* acct proving her wrong. She refused to get her supervisor for him until he finally yells at her. Her supervisor comes on and helps us out in 5 minutes. They'll cancel the collections situation. While I was at the store, I started dealing with their support people on Twitter. That ended with them confirming everything is canceled, I owe nothing and the collections situation will be canceled, and they apologize for the entire situation.  
All of that for me NEVER getting their equipment and NEVER using 1 second of their service. 
Worst company I've ever dealt with. Their phone support people are worthless.

Went and joined T-Moble 4 years ago, got Netflix for getting their service, I took my other son and we got him a phone, bring more business, well they tell me later it changed my plan, they never told me when they were selling me their products!, ok so I explain this to a asst Mgr. When I needed a new phone, he says come in and I'll re-hook your Netflix also, 2 days later I go in and ask for him, he's not there, the Mgr says no way, I show him the link he sent me, it does not work, we won't honor it, so I get a bit upset, they ask me to leave as I call them liars, next I get a letter from the Corporate lawyer, telling me not to go bk to that store, this Company does not know Service, ONLY SALES, and anyone who's been in business and successful knows you MUST have both and they don't and only care about sales. I'm don, i'll pay more for service.

The worst ever! Never signal, dropped calls and wifi sucks!!!!

Why pay for services we are not getting! Contracted in for 2 horrible years... we can't just pay phones and get out if it and they raised our bill without warning. They said they can raise it anytime they want to.

They said they've been working on the towers for months now no difference at all.

They said they would probate our bill for service not provided.
Lies all lies

Wish we could all get together and Sue them for service not provided

Whatever you do ... don't go to t-mobile
The T mobile store at 2883 S. Oneida St., Green Bay, WI, 54304 is very unprofessional, the staff just reset my phone without asking me and was not even able to solve the issue.

T-mobile was recently hacked. mobile says it offers 2years free Mcaffee protection to their customers but obtaining that protection is difficult. I have now spent more than 6 hours on the phone with T-mobile and still cannot get Mcaffee. 
  first off you must request Mcaffee service and then you have to wait (now more than 5 days) for an email to activate this service. 
  I believe T-mobile compromised my personal data as I am now receiving calls from people saying I owe them money for loans I never made and never attempted to make. 
  they have my phone number, email address and social security number.
  I should NOT have had to request remedy from T-mobile and I should not have had to wait (now more than 5 days) or waste several HOURS trying to obtain mcaffee.
  Inexcusable. terrible customer service to a longtime customer.

T-Mobile is the worse company out there. I spent 900 on a phone which they didn't have any protective cases for my phone, I tried multiple times but never did get any in. I have insurance on the phone and they told me if I dropped it, it would be covered. Now they want 100 to fix it which I can pay 100 to the little company in the mall and not pay insurance each month. Plus it would not of happened if they carried the accessories for each phone they sell their customers. Soon as I can I will be leaving tmobile along with both my son's and a few friend's. What a rip off!!! I'd give 0 stars if I could... They sales people are lying to their customers just because they get commission on each phone they sell. Which is bs

I spent 3hrs trying to get my phone to be unlocked. At the end nothing happened. How ridiculous! They told me that my phone is got unlocked but when I tried a different gsm.it does not work. So I called 611 again and I explained to them everything and they said we gonna fix it but again nothing happened.  Very very poor customer service! 😡
I've attempted to speak or text with a representative and each time I'm told there's a 2 hr hold time. This is ridiculous.

This is what I sent to the board members, I’m very disgusted with the horrible service. 
Good morning Mr. Cano, 
I do not normally do things such as this but I am  very unhappy with the service I have been provided by T-Mobile. I have been a customer with Sprint since 2006 and have had no issues with service. Recently, I received a new SIM card, ever since then I have had dropped calls no internet and I’m unable to text. I called your technical support line on 9/07/2021 where I was told I would be sent new sprint SIM cards for my four phones ( after about 2 1/2 hours out of my day) I work in a school and I need service on my phone due to needed certain apps. I was then told that a manager was listening in and wanted to speak with me. The manager apologized to me and asked me how much of a refund I thought I would want . I said 300.00 he said I don’t see a problem told me within 24-48 hours. Well it is now 9/14/2021. And my family has been with almost no service on our phones. I called back today and spoke with a girl named nicole who wanted to charge me for the SIM cards, refused to give my any of the others names that I spoke with prior so that I can speak to them to straighten out our prior conversation. I would number one just like to inform you of your staff and number two like some sort of help, my family and I need proper service on our phones or we will have to find a new supplier.

I was trying to set up a payment plan through a T-Mobile representative he asked me for my account # and routing # and he started that the money would be coming out my account on a Specific day that I requested which he lied the same day I gave my Account # and routing # to the T- Mobile representative an hour later I got a text saying me T- Mobile bill was paid and my baking account was all screwed up

I spent 4 hrs trying to get our PAID phone to be unlocked.  How ridiculous!  They said the number, which we have, is in someone else’s name. Then the nice lady from the Philippines said she could not unlock the phone number that we now have through AT&T because someone else has the number, we sat there explaining we had the same number thru there service for over twenty years.  Very very poor customer service!

I've been with T-mobile for almost 9 years and I can honestly say all around it sucks for the last couple of years. To many hours on technical service calls, always having replace money where I've been overcharged and I mean quite a bit at one time in a 12 month period. This is a frequently happening issue if you don't catch it, they will keep on and tell you they are only able to go back so many months. It doesn't matter that they have over charged you more months. Always having to do call about the same issues. Always having to repeat yourself in 1 call. T-mobile is buying up the market so there's only a few other reputable companies to move to. At their stores the agents doesn't really want to be bothered unless you are buying something. The warranty replacement phones doesn't always work properly nor long for long periods. Agents can tell you that they have gotten a phone back, however they will continue to charge you for the phone no matter what. Please check your billing statement monthly, and be able to tell them what, when and where and agents names. They are never wrong and will act as if conversations with agents never happened. It's that serious about documentation when dealing with T-mobile. Phone constantly saying T-mobile app has stopped working. Spent an hour with tech service, but  30 minutes later it's the exact same pop up.

crap cant game or watch videos. the only way you are getting 100mbps with this is if everyone around the world died and you had full service to yourself. plz if anyone reads this DO NOT BUY WAST OF TIME -10/10. even people that use it once a day would through it out the window. again DO NOT BUY

Run! 3 years ago, I would have recommended them, but, now, the only good thing is that the base plans are cheap. If you, actually, want to use them though....
They lie about what you get. That is after you've spent an hour or more trying to get thru to someone. When you do, there's a good chance the person won't understand you. I don't care what anyone's first language is, or what country they are working out of, but when I call customer service, I need to be able to tell them what the problem is. Latest lie was that I could switch to link zone without changing my plan and still get the same amount of data. Well, after I bought the device I find out I need to upgrade for an extra $30/month. They won't mail me a return label and because of the prior lie they told me, I can't print the emailed label. Then the guy I'm being remarkably polite to, given what's happening, tells me "you need to work with us"....seriously?!

So I got the Samsung S21 Ultra. After just over the 14 day period, I started getting the water drop and can't charge issues. I did a warranty exchange. Naturally, I was charged $5.00 for the exchange. Less than a week later, sim card errors and then the water drop yet again. I called tech support to complain and was assured that my issues would be addressed. I was told a supervisor would call me the next day to authorized an exchange for a different model of phone since I said I'm not going to replace a $1200 phone for another one. Never heard back. 3 days later, called again and again, was told "I'm sorry" about 35 times and that I would hear from a supervisor. Again, nothing. I went into the local store and was ignored fort 20 minutes before my presence was acknowledged and checked in. After getting tired of waiting, I came back the next day only to get greeted the same way. 
 I called Monday night and yet again had to explain. A supervisor called an hour later and after telling him that I was going to drop them after 17 years, he assured me that he was on it and I would get this all rectified. Nothing. I sent text message last night and was told the same lines that were read to me off their cue cards by so many others. Now this isn't the first time this has happened. Every single phone I've ever gotten from T-Mobile has had to be replaced through warranty. Every one. Google, Apple, One Plus, and now Samsung. My wife just got a new phone and so we are going to take this and hers and drop them off at the store and say goodbye. I would rather pay a little more with Verizon or someone else but have better customer service. I don't know how much money I've spent $5 are a time for replacements as well as my $50 per month for charges for new phones that die, drop calls and can't charge.
Terrible Customer Service

Nothing is as good as it seems. That’s one of those phrases a dad might spout off when the 2-for-1 coupon doesn’t work. And yet, I catch myself saying it now. Saying it? That’s not quite accurate. More like: spitting it out through clenched teeth as my call drops for the third time while my wife in on a major interstate in Connecticut. NOTHING IS AS GOOD AS IT SEEMS. Deep breath.

Damn you, T-Mobile. You lured me in with hot pink maps, gushing with 4G and 5G coverage, and sweet whispers in my ear that made my neck hair tingle, (“Oh yeah baby, I’ll pay off your iPhone”). I didn’t even know I had neck hair before you. Your agents on chat had me at Carrier Freedom. Hell, the plan would end up being even cheaper than my previous carrier! You were one hot package. 

Until you weren’t.

Like any feeble dating app hookup, it was a complicated affair that left us both cold and dissatisfied at the end of the night. Even getting this relationship started was complicated- you told me some older phones would work without issue on your network, which turned out to be false. I stood awkwardly as my two carriers pointed fingers at each other- “It's her fault!” “No it's not, he doesn’t know what he’s talking about!”. It was an awkward moment and I wish I could have left you both standing there in the rain. But, fear not, I resolved the issues after many trips to various storefronts and drawn out phone calls. 

So, fine. We got it squared away. I figured it would be worth it, after all, nothing worthwhile is ever easy! I paid off my final bill and we started holding hands in public. Except…now that we were free to be together, you kept letting go? Every time I tried to connect, you would ghost me. I looked down to see “call failed” more times in one day than I had seen in YEARS with my first…carrier. Okay I thought, every new relationship has to work through some kinks. We will get through this.

But you continued to stand me up. At the park. At the office. At HOME where nobody would even see us. You sure look like you’re ready, two bars! Three! But no streaming. I'm not even trying for YouTube at this point, I’m just trying to load something romantic on Spotify for us to enjoy together. To rekindle the flame and bring back what we could have had. But it’s just not working.

Where did we go wrong? Was it me? Did I jump too quickly, drawn in by your sumptuous marketing? Or was it you? Making big promises that you couldn’t hold up? You looked the part, standing there with full bars on your profile, but it was all a con. 

Well, now we are in this together. We barely hold hands anymore. I’m ashamed to admit that I talk about you to my friends, openly confessing that yeah, you’re still good looking, but our connection is long gone. You're dead inside. 

And it’s too bad because I actually like your friends- the ones at the store and online and on the phone- we really got to know each other. And they were really patient and kind (even if a little misinformed at times). But they can’t fill the cold void in my heart when I go to sleep at night next to you. 

Two bars. One regret.

My old carrier didn’t have great friends but at least our connection was secure, I could RELY on her. I knew she’d be there for me when I needed to talk, or laugh. It wasn’t even a question. But that’s gone now. All I can hope is that maybe she’ll take me back someday, though I know it will be painful for all of us. I know there will be more shouting, more finger pointing, maybe we will even lose some of the pictures we took together. I never quite know what's going to happen when it comes to you. You're overrated and volatile. 

I’m done trying. My family doesn’t even like you. We tolerate you. Your occasional attempts to reconnect aren’t going to do anything but drive the knife deeper into my chest. 

The only good thing from this? You helped me realize what I had in my first carrier. She wasn’t at sexy, her profile picture is a little dated, but she was there for me. And I want that back. 

I guess what I'm saying is this: it's not me, it's you.

I’m ready to let go. Are you? I'd like to end this amicably. 

But something tells me you’re not going to let me go so easily.

Let me start by saying that we had no options but to switch to T-Mobile. We were with Sprint for about twenty years and because they were bought out we switched. Also, they literally took about 4.5 hours at the T-Mobile store to get us the new phones and set them up…horrible night. Services sucks! 5G?!?!? What’s that? I think 3G worked better!!! Customer service is horrible. 1+ hour waiting time (both, phone and chat). Stay away, seriously they are awful!

SPRINT CUSTOMERS  - RUN, DON'T WALK AND FIND ANOTHER CARRIER LIKE PATRIOT MOBILE OR ONE OF THE SMALLER GUYS; DO NOT SWITCH TO T-MOBILE YOU WILL BE HIGHLY DISAPPOINTED!!!!!.  I went to the store to have my T-Mobile Z64 hotspot activated, it needed a new SIM card and phone number.  They got in to work but not on the internet.  McKinney said they didn't know why.  At least they were honest.  Went to T-Mobile on Stacy, they told be because it wasn't LTE that it was no good.  What they didn't say is that it was no good on THEIR NETWORK.  Then I asked for a pay-as-you-go plan because that is what i had before.  The decided they should GIVE me a different hotspot but it was free and the service was $10 a month but my plan was a little cheaper than my Sprint so it was more like $7/month.  What they failed to tell me was there is a 2-YEAR AGREEMENT attached to "FAKE FREE HOTSPOT" and that the return policy was 14 days.  I was there on the 15th day with the Hotspot and they would not cancel or take it back.  My other Hotspot works fine with other carriers.  I was sold something I didn't need, they won't take it back, they won't cancel the contract I was not informed about, they won't give me any kind of credit on my bill for all this trouble and being lied to and talking to the technical support who didn't even know what a hotspot separate from my phone was.  I am astounded at their lack of help and knowledge and the # of people I was transferred to that still could/would not help.  This never happened with Sprint.  I feel like I've stepped 20 years into the past with "Can you Hear Me Now".   After the 2 years are up with the hotspot I don't need, it will have cost me $168 only because my plan is a little less but would have really cost $240 and they robbed me of 20Gig from my Sprint phone Hotspot, that they didn't tell me about till after they switched it up on T-Mobile.  I told them I wanted my account switched back to Sprint.  They said they couldn't do it; wouldn't do it!  As a matter of fact this God awful, drop calls 17x times yesterday and too many other days in the last 2 weeks, 20+ hours on phone with technical support and customer service over a 2-week period, my account is still not online,  Given all kinds of excuses about that and how it takes them time to switch it over, but I already have a bill I cannot see.  Their verification method requires you use a code from the store which I had, that does not work.  I cannot even sign up for my own account that is on AutoPay.  Can't even see it.  Keep getting told to go back to the store.  Can you believe this crap!!!!!!  They deleted all my VM from my mom who died in March and didn't say a word about how your saved voice mails would be deleted.  They activated the Visual Voice Mail and I never asked them to do it which further caused the deletion of the rest of my voice mails.  All their service sucks plus my 4 trips to the store in Allen 2x and the store in Mckinney 2x.   I hate T-Mobile!!!!!! I am reporting them to BBB.  LIARS! AWFUL CUSTOMER SERVICE, AWFUL TECH SUPPORT, I WILL SAY THE PEOPLE IN THE STORE WERE NICE AND TRIED TO HELP BUT THEY ARE ALSO THE ONES WHO TOLD ME IN ALLEN THAT MY HOTSPOT WOULD NOT WORK ANYMORE.  THAT IS A LIE.  LIARS, LIARS, LIARS!!!!!!!!!!!!!

I made four phone calls after their client data breach, TMobile failed completely to clarify the extent of their security failure or rectify increasing breaks in my cell phone service. First call placed by me I was cut off (one of the issues I have been experiencing since the breach). 2nd phone call was 21 minutes of talk inflicted upon me by their 'expert' that was total gaslighting. So concerned, we understand on and on in English so difficult to understand I asked for a supervisor. 'Expert' returned call and again gave me no clarification or assurances. I must assume at this point that all of my information has been disseminated and I am totally vulnerable. 
4th call I received after 5 PM MST was a disturbing repeat of gibberish and the avoidance of providing ANY clarification. I urge anyone with this provider to immediately pursue coverage with another company, CEASE any use of a Tmobile connection that involves the necessity of security and report their absolute failure to provide Secure service to their customers to the applicable State and Federal entities.

***Buyer Beware*** this is an honest review pertaining to my experience with this T-Mobile store. I went to this store to get my daughters phone replaced since it wasn’t working properly. Upon entering we were told that we HAD to switch from Sprint to T-Mobile (because of the merger). And that he could get us NEW phones for only $12 each per month. Sounds like a great deal right? After sitting down with the T-Mobile rep (who was less than friendly) & half an hour or so into the transaction he asked for my credit card because we HAD to pay off my husbands phone before we switched to T-Mobile. So I paid over $350 to do so (because remember we HAD to switch to T-Mobile from Sprint before the end of the year). I handed over my credit card a total of 3 times and walked out of the store having paid almost $650. Wait where was the fact that we were told we only had to pay 12.00 each per month for the new phones without any other costs being revealed until further in the transaction? I was so confused (I have a health condition which effects the way I process information). So I had someone with me who could vouch for the information that I am giving in this review. When I realized what had happened I called corporate at T-Mobile & was told I DID NOT HAVE TO SWITCH TO T-MOBILE FROM SPRINT. In fact I was told they didn’t have a date for the roll out & there wasn’t a timeline in effect for those of us still on Sprint. We didn’t have to switch at all. BUT WAIT IT GETS BETTER, the rep told us my mother in law qualified for a free phone (she is also on our plan). I questioned the rep about this because she lives in Greenville & is 90 years old. And I asked him how she would go about doing this & he said he made a note in the system and all she would have to do is go to a T-Mobile store & get “her free phone.” When she arrived I spent an hour and a half with the T-Mobile store in Greenville & there wasn’t any notation in the system stating she qualified for a free phone. In fact, she didn’t get the free phone that we were PROMISED, a 90 year old woman with a fixed income was told she had to pay $632. to get a “free phone”. Needless, to say she was totally taken advantage of. Imagine a 90 year old woman on a fixed income paying out $632. which impacts her living expenses. She was so upset & we sent her the $632. so that she could buy groceries. Just wanting to switch out a phone for my daughter cost me $1300. Trust me I spoke with the assistant store manager the day after we left T-Mobile & explained that we just went into the store to switch out a phone and ended up leaving paying $100’s more. How is that a deal T-Mobile? Shame on you! It took me weeks to finally speak with the Assistant store manager for a follow-up. And the store manager? She protected her employee, she tried to convince me that it was merely my perception of the events. Perception? Well my perception cost me $1300. HEY LAUREN, instead of blaming the customer and GRILLING them on what they didn’t understand (you are worse then Judge Judy). How about you deal in facts? We were told we HAD to switch which in fact wasn’t true. My 90 year old mother in law didn’t get a “free phone” in fact the  resolution you offered was to have my 90 year old mother-in-law go back to the store in Greenville to finance a phone we ended up paying for.  Lauren, you were rude, unhelpful and determined to protect your employee. And In the end can we go back to Sprint and get all our money back? Of course not, the only way she would help us was to have us turn in all our devices to get a PARTIAL refund. How in the world does T-MOBILE think what they did was in our best interest? Knowing how much we were going to have to pay why would you tell us we had to switch when in fact had we stayed the way we were we wouldn’t have had to pay $1300. And spend hours trying to resolve this. Shame on you Lauren & T-Mobile. For taking advantage of a 90 year old woman & long term customers. If you read this far please don’t let yourself be taken advantage of and go to a DIFFERENT carrier.

I have been a T-Mobile customer for many years, mainly due to the free international 2G data. Last year, I stopped by a T-Mobile store, to look at new phones. I didn't at the time upgrade, but the representative looked at my account, and said I could save a little on my monthly bill if I switched plans. My only question was if this new plan would still include the free international data. I was assured that it would. It turned out that it didn't, and I didn't find this out until my family was traveling, and unable to use their phones. I contacted T-Mobile many times, but the result was that they would not be responsible for what I was told by their own representative in their own store. Bottom line - don't trust what you're told in a T-Mobile store. Get it in writing and review it before agreeing to anything. If their representative misrepresents something, it is the customer's responsibility.
